title: 3‑2‑1 Backup Rule Explained: Protect Your Data from Disaster
summary: The 3-2-1 backup rule is a data resiliency strategy that specifies maintaining three copies of data across two different media types with one copy stored offsite. This approach protects against data failures and ransomware attacks through geographic and media diversity.
writeup: This IBM Technology video explains the 3-2-1 backup rule, a data resiliency strategy built on keeping three copies of data, storing them across two different media types, and placing one copy offsite. It matters because the combination of media diversity and geographic separation guards against data failures and ransomware attacks. The video connects the rule to disaster recovery and immutability, framing it as a baseline approach for protecting data against loss and tampering.
